instruction:
The following numbers represent values of the dependent variable that corresponds to equally spaced values of the independent variable. Classify these sequences as linear, exponential or neither:
$\begin{array}{cc}\hline sequence & classification \\ \hline 1, 2, 4, 8, 16,... & [ANS] \\ \hline-6,-8,-10,-12,-14,... & [ANS] \\ \hline 2, 4, 6, 8, 10,... & [ANS] \\ \hline-1, 0, 2, 5, 9,... & [ANS] \\ \hline 1, 4, 16, 64, 256,... & [ANS] \\ \hline 1, 16, 81, 256, 625,... & [ANS] \\ \hline \end{array}$
output: EXPONENTIAL, LINEAR, LINEAR, neither, exponential, neither
